hq: Not disclosed (no physical offices; all data processed on cloud services)
signing_authority: Not completed (signature field left blank in report)

system_description.overview: Salv AI Inc. operates the Tenzo platform, an AI-automated compliance management platform that helps companies achieve and maintain compliance with frameworks such as SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, ISO 27001, and PCI DSS. The platform leverages AI to automate evidence gathering, perform continuous compliance monitoring, conduct code scanning, infrastructure monitoring, automated security questionnaire completion, and access control management.

products.Automated Compliance Management: End-to-end compliance automation for SOC 2, HIPAA, ISO 27001, GDPR, PCI DSS; integration and monitoring within client environments; continuous compliance monitoring and gap identification; automated evidence collection
products.AI-Powered Security Features: Automated code scanning for security vulnerabilities; automated infrastructure scanning; access control monitoring and alerts; security questionnaire automation
products.Compliance Support Services: Compliance implementation onboarding and setup; ongoing compliance expert guidance and auditor coordination

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out - AWS controls excluded from scope)
regions: Not specified by name; multi-availability zone deployment mentioned
infrastructure.physical_presence: None - no physical offices; no physical servers
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (report states 'multiple availability zones' and 'spinning up multiple servers across all availability zones in AWS')

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in AWS VPC with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality.
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS deployed via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings. Intrusion detection system monitors for unauthorized access and anomalies.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN. VPC VPN connection shown in network diagram.
network_architecture.tls: TLS used for all web-based communications; certificates from established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; endpoint protection includes e-mail scanning
network_architecture.topology: Internet traffic flows through VPC internet gateways into segmented private VPC subnets (WEST and EAST with multiple AZs). Remote admin access via VPC VPN gateway and IPSec VPN tunnel to bastion host. Production HA, Dev, Test, and Demo environments within private subnets.

application_architecture.pattern: Not specified (likely SaaS platform; no explicit architecture pattern disclosed)
compute: EC2 (inferred from 'virtual servers' in VPC description and 'Prod HA' servers in network diagram)
application_architecture.environments: At minimum: Production (HA), Development, Test, Demo - all within AWS VPC private subnets

databases: Production database mentioned (access controls tested); specific DB technology not named
data_storage.backup_frequency: Daily (CC7.4.2: 'Backups are taken daily, multi-availability zones are configured')

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS IAM (passwords controlled through 'Password policy of AWS')
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to production platform; MFA required for source code tool access; MFA required for email access
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: AWS password policy enforced: periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements, account lockout after limited failed attempts

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: AWS KMS (cloud provider key management service)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Git-based (source code management tool with branch protection; 'master branch' referenced)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to require additional approval for every merge request moving change to production environment
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configurations, implementation, and rollback capability
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO; monthly patch management reviews via ITLC meetings; workstations scanned for patch compliance daily; automatic OS updates configured

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (monitoring tool generates alerts at predefined thresholds)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled for admin activities, logon attempts, data deletions, function changes, security configurations, permissions, and roles at application and infrastructure level
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts to IT management for high and critical risk events; change-management/ticketing application for tracking

third_party_services.purpose: Data center / cloud infrastructure - subservice organization (carve-out)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and malware protection installed on all production servers (continuous scanning, weekly AV definition updates) and Mac workstations/laptops
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; internal IT team conducts scans per audit calendar
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party; high/critical findings remediated and tracked via incident management process

untestable: CC2.2.3 - No significant personnel changes during engagement period (control could not be tested)
CC3.2.1 / CC3.1 (cybersecurity insurance) - No cyber incidents during engagement period
CC6.5.2 - No customer terminations during engagement period
CC7.3.1 - No security incidents reported during engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access controls: AWS responsible for physical security of data center; excluded from scope
A1.2 - Environmental protections/backup/recovery: Tenzo has no office/operates from working space managed by building management; all environmental security managed by AWS
notable_controls: Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) established with charter reviewed annually and meetings at least semiannually
IT Leadership Committee (ITLC) meets weekly to review security commitment activities
Quarterly access reviews for all user accounts including privileged accounts
Source code access restricted to authorized users with MFA
Baseline configurations retained as code with configuration management tool for rollback

infrastructure.rationale: AWS-based with multi-AZ deployment and VPC/ACL segmentation. Daily backups confirmed. No multi-region failover mentioned. CDN not mentioned. Specific AWS services (EC2, RDS, etc.) not named - limited technical specificity. No IaC tool named explicitly.

application_architecture.rationale: Separate environments (Prod HA, Dev, Test, Demo) confirmed. Branch protection and code review enforced. However, no programming languages, frameworks, or specific architecture pattern disclosed. Very limited technical transparency.

data_layer.rationale: Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration confirmed. Data classification policy exists. Per-tenant data segregation stated. However, specific database technologies, caching layers, and storage services not named. No data warehouse mentioned.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen test, monthly vulnerability scans, IDS/IPS, firewalls, antivirus on all endpoints, MFA enforced, AWS KMS encryption, cybersecurity insurance, quarterly access reviews. No WAF explicitly mentioned. No named SIEM.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection enforced, separate environments, emergency change process, monthly patch reviews with automatic patching, BCDR tested annually. CI/CD tool not named, IaC tool not specifically named, container orchestration not mentioned.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually. Annual testing performed. Daily backups with multi-AZ. Multi-AZ failover by spinning up servers across availability zones. No RTO/RPO disclosed. No multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only AWS identified as a subservice organization. No other vendors named in report. Single cloud provider with no backup cloud. Extremely low vendor diversity disclosure - the system description content appears to be largely template-based (Tenzo is itself a compliance platform using Tenzo-style report).

overall.rationale: Salv AI / Tenzo demonstrates solid security process maturity (annual pen test, MFA, IDS, quarterly access reviews, BCDR testing) and clean SOC 2 Type 2 opinion with no exceptions. However, the report contains significant template content (highlighted yellow instructions not removed, sample network diagram with placeholder text, unsigned management assertion) suggesting this is an early-stage compliance report. Technical specificity is low - no named CI/CD tools, databases, languages, or frameworks. The company is itself a compliance automation platform using what appears to be their own Tenzo template.

red_flags: Management assertion signature page left blank ('Your Name Here', 'Signing Authority Name', 'Designation', '[Today's Date]') - report was submitted unsigned
System description contains highlighted yellow template instructions that were not deleted (e.g., 'Instructions: Modify this text...' visible throughout)',
Network diagram is a placeholder sample ('Sample network diagram - Please replace this with your actual network diagram!') - no actual infrastructure diagram provided
Organization chart contains placeholder text ('Sample organization chart - Please replace this with your actual organizational chart')
No specific technology stack disclosed (no databases, languages, frameworks, or CI/CD tools named)
Trust criteria scope includes Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy but company determined these are 'not relevant to the system' - contradicts including them in scope
yellow_flags: Only one subservice organization (AWS) identified - no other third-party vendors named
No WAF mentioned despite operating a web-based SaaS platform
Report appears heavily template-based - Salv AI is itself a compliance automation vendor (Tenzo) that apparently used their own product's report template
No specific monitoring tool or SIEM named
green_flags: Clean SOC 2 Type 2 opinion - no exceptions noted in any tested controls
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
MFA enforced across production platform, source code tools, and email
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
Access revocation within one business day of termination
Risk and Governance Executive Committee established for oversight
key_observations: Salv AI Inc. is the legal entity; Tenzo is the product brand - the company sells compliance automation software and has gone through SOC 2 Type 2 themselves, which is strong signal for a security-first culture

The report is produced by Accorp Partners using what appears to be Tenzo's own compliance report template - this is somewhat circular (compliance platform using its own template) and explains why the template instructions were not fully removed
The 3-month audit period (April 2 - July 2, 2025) is the minimum typical for a SOC 2 Type 2 - this is likely a first Type 2 report

Several controls were marked 'untestable' due to no triggering events during the period (no incidents, no customer terminations, no personnel changes) - this is common in short-period first Type 2 reports and is not a deficiency
The network diagram shows WEST and EAST VPCs with 3 availability zones total, suggesting US-East and US-West regions, though this is a sample diagram
The system description boundary explicitly excludes physical office controls since Tenzo operates fully cloud-native with no physical presence
